# Welcome {.unnumbered}

A conceptual introduction to how computer networks actually work — signals,
frames, IP addressing, routing, TCP/UDP, DNS, TLS, HTTP, performance,
cloud and container networking, and production troubleshooting — written
for curious, technically-adjacent readers with **no networking
certification, no systems-programming background, and no prior
familiarity with the OSI model**.

Most introductions to networking begin with a stack of acronyms or a
seven-layer diagram to memorize. This book builds one cumulative mental
model instead, starting from the smallest possible problem — moving
information between two machines — and following it outward until it
becomes the Internet, the Web, cloud networking, container networking,
and production troubleshooting.

*This is a first complete draft, not a finished, professionally edited
book.*
